mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-run trend rate expands corporate earnings expectations, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month through improved cash flow projections.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth surprises over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tight labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE remains below 5.0 percent for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and CPIAUCSL compress real interest rates, reducing the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and expanding equity multiples.
failure_condition: Real rate compression fails to produce positive SPX forward returns in more than 40 percent of observed instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerating while capacity utilization remains elevated indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive despite consecutive months of declining INDPRO growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment gains exceeding 200,000 reinforce labor market strength, supporting near-term consumer demand and driving positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive skew when monthly payroll gains exceed 200,000 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging past 600,000 units signals robust construction activity and household formation, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months where HOUST remains above 600,000 units.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent federal funds rate hikes and rising unemployment rates indicate a policy error where tightening suppresses economic activity faster than inflation falls, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential mortgage debt outstanding reflects increased household leverage and consumption capacity, which historically supports positive short-term equity performance during early economic recoveries.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS increases during periods of falling unemployment over a 3-year window.
